title: SKCS Edge Build Control Ledger
architecture_policy: Scout is the sole governed sports-truth provider. Edge consumes validated Scout intelligence and owns analysis, scoring, prediction, explanation, publication, users, subscribers, and commercial application state.
database_separation_policy: Scout remains on Neon. Edge/Test remains on Supabase. The systems must not be merged into one database merely to simplify integration.
supabase_storage_policy: Supabase must not become a permanent mirror of Scout raw evidence or historical intelligence. FIP transport and Supabase retention are separate decisions. FIP reference, provenance, hash, and derived Edge outputs are preferred over permanent duplication of Scout evidence.
current_note: Build Edge project-control foundations before resuming Scout FIP intake or mixed-provider runtime removal.

tasks.description: Create the canonical Edge build-control ledger, human dashboard, checker, and tests.
tasks.completion_definition: Edge ledger, human control-center document, checker, npm commands, and ledger tests exist and pass scoped validation.
current_evidence: Edge Control Center v1 bootstrap artifacts defined
Marriage gate and Supabase storage gate are explicit control states
Project sequencing distinguishes startable work from separately approved gated work
open_gaps: Edge Master Project Register not yet built
Full Edge project and runtime inventory not yet registered
tasks.next_action: Inspect and separately approve EPR-001 Edge Master Project Register.
tasks.owner_note: Stephen — govern Edge before resuming the deferred Scout-to-Edge bridge.

tasks.description: Inventory and register all known Edge projects, subprojects, runtime systems, legacy paths, commercial boundaries, storage concerns, and integration work before further architecture changes.
tasks.completion_definition: A complete Edge Master Project Register and Repository Asset Register foundation exist; the project register is synchronized with canonical Edge Control Center sequencing; every tracked repository path is registered or explicitly excluded; every registered tracked asset declares a valid governed_by_control_task_id; unresolved final project ownership may remain as a governed cleanup finding when the asset remains bound to a valid Control Center task and has a non-empty next_validation; non-ignored pre-existing untracked workspace paths are discovered separately from the tracked repository universe and preserved as governed workspace candidate evidence or explicitly excluded with a reason without requiring the underlying file to be committed; known first-party rule and governance authority candidates remain identifiable by review role and are linked in a governed candidate relationship graph or explicitly justified as standalone review candidates; candidate status does not establish CURRENT_AUTHORITY; ignored dependency, environment, and cache paths are excluded from first-party authority candidate discovery; no tracked path is unclassified; and asset integrity does not fail merely because an asset is LEGACY, HISTORICAL_EVIDENCE, NO_CONSUMER, ORPHAN, CONFLICT, UNKNOWN, awaiting final project ownership, or awaiting authority review.
proof_required: Edge Master Project Register exists and validates.
Edge Repository Asset Register exists and validates.
Exact project_id / ledger task ID overlaps preserve canonical Control Center sequencing state.
Deterministic tracked repository asset universe is derived from git ls-files.
Every tracked repository path is registered or explicitly excluded.
Unclassified tracked path count is zero.
Every registered tracked asset has a valid governed_by_control_task_id.
UNRESOLVED final project ownership remains a non-fatal governed cleanup finding only when the asset has a valid Control Center task binding and non-empty next_validation.
Invalid or unknown governed_by_control_task_id remains fail-closed.
Non-ignored untracked workspace candidate discovery is performed separately from tracked-path counting.
The untracked discovery boundary uses git-aware non-ignored path discovery such as git ls-files --others --exclude-standard or an equivalent deterministic Git mechanism.
Every discovered pre-existing untracked workspace candidate is preserved in governed workspace candidate evidence or explicitly excluded with a reason.
Preserving a pre-existing untracked workspace candidate does not require committing the underlying artifact.
tracked_path_count continues to mean tracked repository paths only.

Dependency, environment, cache, and generated package internals such as node_modules, .venv, venv, **pycache**, and .pyc are not promoted into the first-party rule/governance authority candidate universe merely because filenames contain rule or governance terms.
Known first-party rule and governance surfaces remain identifiable as authority-review candidates.

Candidate roles distinguish, at minimum where detected: RULE_OR_AGENT_AUTHORITY_CANDIDATE, GOVERNANCE_POLICY_OR_SPEC, GOVERNANCE_OR_RULE_REFERENCE, RULE_GUIDE_OR_REPORT, DATABASE_RULE_ENFORCEMENT, DATABASE_RULE_ALIGNMENT_MIGRATION, RUNTIME_RULE_OR_GOVERNANCE, RULE_PROOF_TEST, RULE_OR_GOVERNANCE_AUDIT_TOOL.
Candidate-role assignment is a review classification and does not establish CURRENT_AUTHORITY.
Known candidate assets are linked through governed related-asset relationships or an equivalent candidate relationship graph.
A candidate with no relationship edge must be explicitly marked as a standalone review candidate with a non-empty justification.
Relationship edges identify review relationships only and do not assert authority precedence.
Final authority classification remains subject to governed review.
Tests prove tracked-path coverage remains independent from pre-existing untracked candidate discovery.
Tests prove untracked candidate evidence may remain when the underlying artifact is not committed.
Tests prove rule/governance candidates are not automatically promoted to ACTIVE_GOVERNANCE or CURRENT_AUTHORITY.
Tests prove unknown Control Center task bindings remain fail-closed.
Tests prove dependency/environment false positives are excluded from first-party authority candidate discovery.
Tests prove candidate relationship graph integrity fails for missing candidate references, duplicate candidate paths, invalid roles, or invalid relationship endpoints.
Tests prove EPR-001 may close without inventing final ownership for bootstrap-registered legacy/unknown assets.
Asset integrity does not fail merely because an asset is LEGACY, HISTORICAL_EVIDENCE, NO_CONSUMER, ORPHAN, CONFLICT, UNKNOWN, awaiting final project ownership, or awaiting authority review.
No runtime provider removal, Scout/FIP intake, Supabase mutation, prediction-rule change, ACCA-rule change, migration execution, or deployment change occurs as part of EPR-001.
current_evidence: Two-register model (Master Project Register + Repository Asset Register) approved
Repository asset universe coverage law approved
tasks.next_action: EPR-001 foundation closed and TESTED. Preserve the Edge Master Project Register, complete tracked repository asset coverage, governed pre-existing untracked workspace candidate snapshot, governed unresolved final project ownership findings, and rule/governance authority candidate graph as Control Center truth. Do not start ESA-001 or unblock Scout ↔ Edge marriage without separate approval; preserve all blocked gates and fail-closed integrity laws.
tasks.owner_note: Do not invent the Edge backlog from memory; derive it from the repository and current architecture.

tasks.description: Map active, legacy, dead, duplicated, commercial, prediction, storage, AI, provider, scheduler, grading, and deployment surfaces in Edge.
tasks.completion_definition: Edge runtime and project surfaces are classified with reachability and ownership evidence.
proof_required: Writer/reader map for critical Supabase objects
current_evidence: Phase 2 provider inspection already showed the original nine-file inventory was incomplete
tasks.owner_note: This is the Edge equivalent of learning what Scout actually contained before repair.

tasks.description: Define the explicit evidence and approval conditions required before Scout becomes Edge's sole sports-truth provider.
tasks.completion_definition: A tested marriage-gate contract defines required FIP, storage, security, provider-removal, pipeline-integrity, and end-to-end proof conditions.
proof_required: Tests proving incomplete prerequisites cannot clear the gate
open_gaps: No Edge-side marriage gate contract exists
tasks.next_action: Wait for Edge inventory and project registration.
tasks.owner_note: Scout and Edge integration remains explicitly blocked until this gate is separately cleared.

tasks.description: Define and implement the smallest governed Edge intake boundary for the canonical validated Scout Fixture Intelligence Package.
tasks.completion_definition: Edge can receive or read an unmodified validated canonical FIP through one governed fail-closed intake boundary.
current_evidence: Current configured pipeline input still originates from dataProvider.getPredictionInputs()
No committed Edge FIP intake boundary has been found
open_gaps: FIP materialization mechanism must be proven
Transport and retention decisions remain open
tasks.next_action: Do not implement until EPR-001, ESA-001, and EMG-001 sequence permits inspection.
tasks.owner_note: This resumes the intentionally deferred Scout-to-Edge bridge.

tasks.task_name: Supabase Storage and FIP Retention Contract
tasks.description: Define what Edge may retain from Scout/FIP while respecting the 0.5 GB Supabase capacity constraint and avoiding duplication of Scout's Neon evidence store.
tasks.completion_definition: A tested retention policy separates transient FIP processing from permanent Edge storage and defines storage-budget enforcement.
proof_required: Tests or checks proving forbidden Scout evidence mirroring is not introduced
current_evidence: Supabase free-tier storage capacity is constrained to approximately 0.5 GB
Scout already owns governed sports evidence in Neon and Drive
open_gaps: Current Supabase table-size inventory not yet integrated into Edge governance
No canonical FIP retention law exists
tasks.next_action: Register and inventory Supabase storage before approving the FIP bridge.
tasks.owner_note: Transporting a FIP does not mean permanently storing the whole FIP in Supabase.

tasks.description: Map and protect Edge authentication, subscriber, service-role, RLS, admin, secrets, and commercial application boundaries independently from Scout sports data.
tasks.completion_definition: Edge's user/subscriber/commercial security boundary is documented, tested, and isolated from Scout integration credentials and sports-truth transport.
open_gaps: Full Edge security boundary has not been registered in a canonical project-control layer
tasks.owner_note: Scout's lower commercial-data sensitivity does not justify weakening Edge's user-data boundary.

tasks.description: Map and protect raw input, normalization, scoring, contextual adjustment, filtering, direct markets, secondary markets, same-match logic, ACCA construction, and publication.
tasks.completion_definition: Critical Edge prediction surfaces and invariants are registered with tests protecting existing scoring and ACCA behaviour during FIP integration.
proof_required: Regression proof before and after FIP intake
current_evidence: aiPipeline remains the primary Edge analysis/orchestration path
Existing ACCA and prediction logic must be preserved during provider removal
tasks.owner_note: Do not break the existing ACCA engine while changing the origin of sports truth.

tasks.description: Remove reachable Edge sports-truth acquisition only after a governed Scout/FIP intake path can replace required runtime input.
tasks.completion_definition: No reachable Edge runtime path independently acquires canonical sports truth from external sports providers.
current_evidence: Phase 1 public provider routes and provider-heartbeat startup were cut
Phase 2 stopped because getPredictionInputs() still depends on buildLiveData() external acquisition
Additional acquisition surfaces were found outside the original nine-file inventory
open_gaps: FIP intake does not yet exist
tasks.next_action: Remain PARTIAL and blocked until EFI-001 provides a governed Scout input origin.
tasks.owner_note: Do not delete analysis logic merely because acquisition and analysis are currently mixed in the same file.

tasks.description: Govern prediction settlement, grading, accuracy tracking, historical backfill, and system-review feedback as one prediction lifecycle.
tasks.completion_definition: Prediction issue-to-outcome-to-grade-to-accuracy flow is registered, verified, and has one canonical lifecycle map.
current_evidence: Multiple grading, settlement, accuracy, audit, bridge, and backfill scripts are exposed in package.json
open_gaps: Lifecycle ownership and duplicate-path risk not yet inventoried
tasks.owner_note: Treat grading as part of Edge truth-about-its-own-predictions, not Scout sports truth.

tasks.description: Define AI provider, EdgeMind, judge, explanation, and fallback responsibilities while prohibiting AI systems from becoming canonical sports-truth providers.
tasks.completion_definition: AI and inference responsibilities are registered and cannot bypass the governed Scout sports-truth boundary.
proof_required: Proof AI output cannot silently replace canonical sports truth
open_gaps: Current AI-provider and EdgeMind boundaries require inventory
tasks.owner_note: OpenAI, Gemini, or EdgeMind may analyse supplied intelligence; they do not own canonical sports facts.

tasks.description: Register public, authenticated, subscriber, prediction, ACCA, explanation, and admin delivery surfaces.
tasks.completion_definition: Subscriber-facing Edge delivery surfaces and access requirements are registered and traceable to canonical prediction/publication state.
tasks.next_action: Wait for security and pipeline inventory.

tasks.description: Register GitHub, Render, Vercel, Supabase, environment configuration, migrations, schedulers, cron/workflows, and deployment readiness.
tasks.completion_definition: Edge deployment and operational systems are registered with explicit environment, scheduler, migration, and readiness ownership.
current_evidence: Edge package scripts expose sync, refresh, grading, settlement, provider audits, enrichment, pipeline, and Antigravity operational commands
open_gaps: Operational command reachability and current-use status not yet classified
tasks.next_action: Wait for system, storage, and security inventory.
tasks.owner_note: Render suspension/deployment state must not be confused with local runtime correctness.

tasks.description: Prove one governed validated Scout FIP can cross the approved intake boundary, drive Edge analysis, and produce derived Edge output without direct external sports acquisition or unnecessary Scout-data retention in Supabase.
tasks.completion_definition: A controlled end-to-end proof demonstrates Scout-to-Edge FIP intake, validation, idempotency, analysis, provenance linkage, minimal retention, and zero reachable direct external sports acquisition.
tasks.next_action: Remain BLOCKED until all dependencies are satisfied.
tasks.owner_note: This is the final technical proof before the Scout-Edge marriage gate can be separately approved.
